item.text: The current shape confirms a concrete inconsistency: `run_graph_status()` can normalize a closure receipt into a completed state, but `taskflow_continuation` still derives an active bounded unit from `next_node` when status is completed. I’m patching that mismatch and adding focused regression tests around the post-close completed state rather than touching broader dispatch logic.

I’ve narrowed the fix to two coupled invariants. First, `run_graph_status()` needs to stop advertising an open delegated cycle once the active task is closed, even if the persisted execution rows still look mid-lane. Second, continuation binding must not keep pointing at the just-closed task in that reconciled state. I’m patching both together, with a close-task regression test to prove the end-to-end shape.

I’m editing three places only: make `run_graph_status()` collapse an active run into a completed, cycle-clear state when its task is now closed; make continuation binding synthesize `dispatch_target: closure` for that completed shape; and have `close_task()` refresh any matching run bindings immediately so stale task-bound continuation state is replaced on write.

The patch is in. I’ve kept it to the three requested files and added regression tests for the exact post-close invariant: status clears the delegated cycle, continuation rebases to `closure`, and the close-task write path refreshes persisted binding state immediately.
The test run is serialized by Cargo’s build lock, not by code failure. I’m polling the running sessions and will stop as soon as I have the three targeted results.
